title: Legacy Systems

content.statement: You're building for what lasts beyond you.
content.question: What are you building this year that will still matter in 50 years?
content.mental_model: Tree planted today gives shade later.
sop: List your current major projects or investments.
For each, ask honestly: does this stop mattering the moment I stop showing up?
Rate Impact, Duration, and Transferability each 1-10 (see calculation, page 5), how big the effect, how long it lasts, how well it transfers to others.
Pick the lowest-scoring project and redesign it to survive without you: documentation, ownership transfer, or a systemized process.
Choose one long-term asset (brand, IP, a trained team, owned property) to invest in this quarter over a short-term win.
questions: What are you building this year that will still matter in 50 years?
If your business or work stopped requiring you tomorrow, would it keep running, or would it disappear with you?
What are you optimizing for right now: this quarter's cash, or something that outlives you?

definitions.def: Intellectual property; things you own that aren't physical, like a brand, content, trademarks, patents, or software, that can keep producing value without ongoing labor.

content.equation_example: Rate each 1-10: Impact, Duration, Transferability. Impact 8 times Duration 7 times Transferability 5 equals 280. Higher means what you're building keeps creating value long after you stop touching it; lower means it's tied to your ongoing personal effort and ends when you do.
content.closing_statement: Build things that outlive your involvement in them.

caption:
Principle Nº 180: Legacy Systems

You're building for what lasts beyond you.

Ask yourself: What are you building this year that will still matter in 50 years?
